accepted_risks.description: 6 entries in the cross-module table (interfaces.md §6 #2, #5, #6, #7, #13, #14, #15, #16) are covered by unit tests rather than integration tests. These are internal scheduler/portfolio sub-module calls or pure-function reuses; the test-plan correctly judges unit tests sufficient. Shield should nevertheless ensure these interfaces are exercised through their consuming integration tests (e.g., RetrainScheduler integration exercise calls evaluate_factor indirectly).

Architecture §9 lists 23 cross-module entries while interfaces.md §6 lists 26. The 3 additional entries in interfaces.md (#24 v0.1.0 model loading compat, #25 v0.1.0 CLI, #26 LGBMTop20Strategy) are v0.1.0 backward-compat items—appropriately added in interfaces.md §6 as the canonical cross-module list. No contradiction.
